meta.description: LeieXEie — compare renting and buying with Norwegian tax and finance rules. Educational tool, not financial advice.

header.tagline: Your smart guide to choosing whether to rent or own a home in Norway.

inputs.years: Years You Plan to Live There
inputs.bsuActive: I save in BSU (under 34 and first-time buyer)

tooltips.monthlyRent: Your current monthly rent. Used as the baseline cost for the renting scenario.
tooltips.rentIncrease: How much your landlord raises the rent each year. Norwegian average is around 3–5%.
tooltips.purchasePrice: The asking price of the home you want to buy.
tooltips.downPayment: Auto-set to 15% of the purchase price. Norwegian law requires a minimum 15% down payment (10% for first-time buyers under 34).
tooltips.mortgageRate: The annual interest rate on your mortgage. Check with your bank for a current offer.
tooltips.loanTermYears: How many years until your mortgage is fully paid off. 25–30 years is most common in Norway.
tooltips.monthlyHoaFee: Monthly joint costs in a shared housing cooperative — includes maintenance, insurance, and management fees.
tooltips.stampDuty: Automatically set to 2.5% of the purchase price. Set to 0 for new builds or housing cooperatives (borettslag).
tooltips.otherClosingCosts: Other one-time costs at purchase: tinglysning (deed + mortgage registration), bank setup fees, etc. Typically 5,000–15,000 kr.
tooltips.brokerSellingFee: A typical broker fee in Norway is around 100,000 kr or 1–3% of the sale price.
tooltips.sharedDebt: Shared debt in a borettslag that transfers with the unit. Affects total cost and tax deductions.
tooltips.municipalFees: Annual municipal fees covering water, sewage, and waste collection. Typically 10,000–20,000 kr/year.
tooltips.renovationPct: Annual maintenance budget as a percentage of the home's value. A common rule of thumb is 0.5–1% per year.
tooltips.homeInsurance: Annual insurance for the building structure. Usually 5,000–15,000 kr/year depending on size.
tooltips.propertyTax: Property tax charged by some Norwegian municipalities. Many municipalities charge 0%. Check your local rate.
tooltips.hoaFeeIncrease: How much the HOA / shared costs rise each year. Boards typically adjust annually in line with costs.
tooltips.years: How long you plan to stay. The longer you own, the more the upfront buying costs are spread out.
tooltips.appreciationRate: Norwegian average home price growth is approximately 2–4% per year.
tooltips.investmentReturn: Expected annual return on your savings account before tax. Norwegian savings accounts typically yield 3–5% per year. 22% tax on interest is automatically applied.
tooltips.contentsInsurance: Contents insurance covering your belongings. Typically 1,500–3,000 kr/year as a renter.
tooltips.electricity: Annual electricity and heating costs. Varies widely — roughly 15,000–30,000 kr/year for an apartment.
tooltips.internet: Annual broadband cost. Typically around 4,000–6,000 kr/year in Norway.
tooltips.parking: Monthly parking costs if applicable. Leave at 0 if included in rent or not relevant.
tooltips.sharedDebtRate: The interest rate on the housing cooperative's shared debt (fellesgjeld). This interest is included in monthly HOA fees and is also tax-deductible. Typically close to the current mortgage rate.
tooltips.interestOnlyYears: Number of years paying interest only (avdragsfrihet), with no principal repayment. After this period the full loan amortises over the remaining term. Many Norwegian banks offer up to 5 years.
tooltips.inflation: General price growth used to convert future amounts to today's money. Norwegian long-run target is 2%.
tooltips.savingsAccountBalance: Amount kept as a liquid buffer in a savings account. Interest is taxed annually at 22% (automatically applied).
tooltips.savingsAccountRate: Interest rate on your savings account. Check with your bank. 22% tax on interest is automatically applied.
tooltips.askBalance: Amount you have invested in an Aksjesparekonto (ASK). Grows tax-free until withdrawal — 37.84% capital gains tax is modelled automatically.
tooltips.askRate: Expected annual return on shares/funds in an Aksjesparekonto (ASK). Historical average ~7% per year. Capital gains tax of 37.84% (Norwegian shareholder model) is applied only on withdrawal — modelled automatically.
tooltips.askShieldingRate: The shielding deduction (skjermingsfradrag) reduces taxable ASK gains. Skatteetaten sets the rate annually (typically 3–5%). Only gains above accumulated shielding are taxed at 37.84%.
tooltips.bsuActive: BSU (Boligsparing for ungdom): save up to 27,500 kr/year and get a 10% tax deduction (max 2,750 kr/year). Available until age 34 if you haven't used BSU for a purchase. The deduction reduces the renter's effective cost.
tooltips.bsuYearlyContribution: Amount saved in BSU this year. Max 27,500 kr (2024). You get 10% back as a tax deduction.

footer.disclaimer: For educational purposes only. Not financial advice.

recommendation.buyDesc: After {{years}} years, owning puts you {{amount}} ahead. Your net proceeds after selling the home come to {{equity}}, versus the {{portfolio}} you'd have built by renting and investing the difference.
recommendation.rentDesc: After {{years}} years, renting and investing puts you {{amount}} ahead. Your investment portfolio of {{portfolio}} grows larger than the {{equity}} you'd walk away with after selling the home.
recommendation.breakevenAt: The two paths cross at year {{year}} — before that, the other option was ahead.
recommendation.noBreakevenBuy: Buying stays ahead for the entire {{years}}-year period.
recommendation.noBreakevenRent: Renting stays ahead for the entire {{years}}-year period.
recommendation.realTermsNote: All figures are in today's money — adjusted for inflation.
recommendation.disclaimer: This is an estimate based on your inputs and assumptions about future market conditions. Always consult a qualified financial advisor before making major financial decisions.

breakdown.title: Calculation Details
breakdown.initialInvestmentNote: Invested in the market instead of buying (down payment + closing costs)
breakdown.securityDepositNote: locked, not invested — returned at move-out
breakdown.taxNote37: 22% tax on interest (savings account rate) applied automatically
breakdown.monthlyDiffNote: Monthly diff (buyer − renter) added to / deducted from portfolio
breakdown.tableNote: Net worth figures are in real terms (inflation-adjusted). Bold value is leading that year.
breakdown.wealthTaxHome: Primary residence: 25% of market value up to 10 MNOK
breakdown.wealthTaxHomeTiered: Primary residence above 10 MNOK: 70% of the excess
breakdown.taxFreeHomeSale: Capital gain on home sale is tax-free (primary residence, owned & lived in 12 of last 24 months)
breakdown.shieldingNote: Shielding deduction ({{rate}}%/yr) has been applied to ASK gains
breakdown.bsuBenefit: BSU deduction: {{amount}} kr/yr reduces renter's effective cost
breakdown.askCapitalGainsTax: ASK capital gains tax after shielding (37.84%)
